AGENT_HANDOFF_PHASE_1A.title: Agent Handoff — Phase 1A
AGENT_HANDOFF_PHASE_1A.content:
Phase 1A Handoff: Thorpe Vault Bootstrap
Overview
This phase established a new standalone Thorpe family research vault, imported initial source files from Butch, and created base navigation/reference/person pages for source-driven expansion.
What Changed

Created a dedicated Quartz vault for Thorpe research.
Configured project metadata for thorpe-family-research.
Imported the initial source package from C:\Users\zach\Desktop\Thorpe.
Added foundational wiki pages and indexing structure.
Added project agent guidance files (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md).

Files Created or Updated
Core project files

package.json (homepage/repository updated)
quartz.config.ts
.github/workflows/deploy.yml (removed Copley timeline copy step)
.gitignore

Content pages

index.md
Home.md
People Directory.md
Search Index.md
CHANGELOG.md
Topics/Thorpe Pedigree Timelines.md
References/Butch Thorpe Email.md
People/Robert Butch Thorpe.md

Agent guidance and handoff

CLAUDE.md
GEMINI.md
AGENTS.md
AGENT_HANDOFF_PHASE_1A.md

Raw source imports

References/raw/butch-thorpe-email.txt
References/raw/PedigreeTimelines2025.cdr
References/raw/PedigreeTimelines2019Descendants2.cdr

Verified Facts from Sources
From References/raw/butch-thorpe-email.txt:

Butch provided pedigree timeline attachments.
Timeline bars represent ancestor lifespans.
Red dots indicate official records with numbered references to Butch’s copies.
Blue dots indicate census records.
Not all ancestors are shown; only early generations that fit page space.
Butch can provide a PDF catalog of record types.

Known Limitations

The .cdr files are not directly transcribed in this environment.
Person-level extraction from those files is pending export to PDF/image.
Current person-level data is intentionally minimal to avoid unsourced claims.

Build/Test Status

npm ci completed
npm test passed
npm run build passed
npm run check reports broad formatting warnings inherited from copied Quartz source files

Git/GitHub Status

Repository created: github.com/zcopley/thorpe-family-research
Branch: main
Initial commit pushed: 74744eb

Recommended Next Steps

Export PedigreeTimelines2025.cdr and PedigreeTimelines2019Descendants2.cdr to PDF/image.
Request Butch’s record-type PDF and numbered-reference legend.
Begin structured extraction into People/ profiles with explicit source citations.
Add generation taxonomy once enough relationships are transcribed.

Session Addendum — 2026-04-22 Shared Intake Crawl
Scope
Performed external source reconnaissance against G:\My Drive\Thorpe Shared Intake (read-only crawl) to identify ingest-ready evidence and extraction priorities.
What Changed in This Session

No new genealogical claims were committed to People/ pages in this session.
Repository tracking artifacts were updated:

CHANGELOG.md updated with intake-crawl session note.
This handoff file updated with intake findings and action queue.



Files Reviewed (External Intake)

Obituaries/Obituaries of Relatives/OBITUARY Ronene Shelton.txt
Certificates/Certificate Images/CERT0058PalmerWilliaHenry-Death Record.txt
Certificates/Certificate Images/Parish Registers/JamesKellyBaptism1830.txt
Certificates/Certificate Images/Parish Registers/WilliamKellyBurial1840.txt
Certificates/Certificate Images/Parish Registers/JohnKirbyAliceMeadowsMarriage1752.txt
Certificates/Certificate Images/Parish Registers/WilliamEmblowKeziaKirbyMarriage1786.txt
Census/CensusImages/British/EnglishCensusCitations.txt
Census/CensusImages/British/English Census Notes.txt
Census/Old files/Ancestors in the Census.txt
Pedigree Timeline/Misc Pedigree Timeline Files/SPICLINE.txt
Parsed content supplied during session for 2025 pedigree timeline pages and CensusSummaryIndividual pages.

Verified Items from Reviewed Sources

Parish register extracts include directly citable entries for Kelly/Kirby/Emblow lines with dates, places, and repository links.
Death index note confirms an indexed death record entry for William Henry Palmer (Waterloo, Iowa, 8 May 1927).
Census citation notes include UK and US references (HO107/RG9/RG10/RG13 and US roll/ED identifiers) tied to named individuals.
SPICLINE.txt provides a compact generation chain from Peter Spicer through Karen Lu Spicer and Robert AJ Thorpe.
Parsed timeline pages provide person name/date spans and marker categories that align with Butch’s earlier legend (certificate/census/obituary/burial indicators).

Uncertain or Pending Verification

OCR-derived or parsed timeline text may contain transcription and spelling noise; each claim still requires image/PDF cross-check.
Some entries are explicitly approximate (c., ?, before/after) and must remain marked as uncertain in downstream pages.
Living or recently deceased-person details (for example, modern obituaries) require conservative handling and user confirmation before broad publication.

Priority Next Steps After Crawl

Stage intake files into References/raw/inbox/ in batches by source type (Pedigree Timeline, Census, Certificates, Obituaries, BurialSites).
Start text-first extraction from .txt files and parsed timeline outputs into source notes under References/.
Create/expand People/ pages only with source-cited, confidence-labeled facts.
Keep People Directory and Search Index synchronized as new person/topic/reference pages are added.
Add follow-on handoff file when person-level ingestion begins so phase boundaries remain clear.

References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Obituaries/OBITUARY Ronene Shelton.txt
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/BurialSites/BurialSites.pdf

Verified vs Uncertain
Verified in this phase

Staged files exist in repo inbox under the expected date-stamped intake folder.
Parish and death-index text extracts provide directly citable dates/places/names for first-pass profiles.
Spicer lineage text provides a clear chain from Peter Busecot to Karen Lu Spicer and Robert AJ Thorpe.

Uncertain and intentionally deferred

OCR/parsing noise from timeline/census PDF interpretations remains possible.
Some identities require reconciliation across spelling variants (for example Monson/Munson, Thorp/Thorpe, Witfield/Whitfield).
Approximate dates (c., ?, before/after) remain unresolved and were not over-normalized.
Living/recent-person data from modern obituary text was staged as source but not broadly propagated into additional profiles.

Build/Test Status

npm test: pass.
npm run build: pass.
npm run check: pass after excluding generated public/ and .quartz-cache/ artifacts from TypeScript scope and narrowing check to tsc --noEmit.

Prepared commit/push verification flow to confirm GitHub Pages reflects these visibility changes.

Recommended Next Steps

Process CensusSummaryIndividual.pdf in slices and map each summary page to existing/new People/ profiles.
Extract date/place evidence from the four staged 2025 pedigree timeline PDFs and reconcile against SPICLINE.txt.
Create canonical-identity merge notes for spelling variants before larger batch ingestion.
Move fully processed staged files from References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/ to References/raw/processed/ in controlled batches.

Home.title: Home
Home.content:
Thorpe Family Research Vault
Welcome to the Thorpe family research vault. This project is separate from the Copley family vault and is focused on building a source-grounded Thorpe genealogy.
Scope

Build Thorpe family person profiles from primary or near-primary records
Track source provenance and confidence levels
Preserve direct source files and extraction notes
Cross-link to Copley research only where family lines intersect

Initial Sources

Butch Thorpe Email
Thorpe Pedigree Timelines

Deployment Status
Latest deployed update: 2026-04-22 intake batch-1 ingestion.

Changelog
Agent Handoff — Phase 1B
People Directory
Search Index

Next Steps

Sync shared uploads into References/raw/inbox/.
Open and export PedigreeTimelines2025.cdr to PDF or image format.
Open and export PedigreeTimelines2019Descendants2.cdr to PDF or image format.
Request Butch’s PDF catalog of record types and the key for numbered record references.
Start structured extraction of people, dates, places, and relationships into People/ pages.

People-Directory.title: People Directory
People-Directory.content:
People Directory
Confirmed Individuals from Current Source Set
Robert “Butch” Thorpe

Contributor who provided pedigree timeline files and methodology notes.

Andrew Ault

1850 Island Creek Township, Jefferson County, Ohio household extract staged from census-summary pages.

Elizabeth Plomey Ault

Multi-decade US census entries (1850-1920) staged from census-summary pages.

Frederick Ault

1850 Meigs County, Ohio household-head entry staged from census-summary pages.

George Spicer

Staged from shared intake lineage chain as spouse of Hattie Risden.

Hattie Risden

Staged from shared intake lineage chain in the Spicer branch.

James Kelly

Peterborough christening entry extracted from shared intake parish notes.

John Kirby

Oundle parish marriage extract (1752) with later witness linkage in 1786 entry.

Kezia Kirby

Oundle parish marriage extract (1786) linked to Emblow line.

Lenore Hetty Tallman

Daughter entry in 1880 and 1900 Tallman households staged from census-summary pages.

Lester Harold Spicer

Staged from shared intake lineage chain as spouse of Ruby Bernice Prior.

Miller Mathias Tallman

Head-of-household entries across 1880-1920 Iowa census extracts in census-summary pages.

Ruby Bernice Prior

Staged from shared intake lineage chain in the Prior branch.

William Emblow

Oundle parish marriage extract (1786) with witness details pending image-level validation.

William Henry Palmer

Death-index extract staged from shared intake certificates notes.

William Kelly

Identified as father in James Kelly christening extract and probable match to 1840 burial entry.

Pending Extraction
Additional Thorpe family members are present in the staged intake PDFs under References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/ and in legacy .cdr files that still require structured transcription.

People/Andrew-Ault.title: Andrew Ault
People/Andrew-Ault.content:
Andrew Ault
Biographical Profile

Name: Andrew Ault
Role in this project: Direct ancestor listed in the census-summary index and early US census table extracts.

Source-Cited Facts

A census-summary entry gives Andrew Ault as born 2 Apr 1769 and died 28 Mar 1852.
The 1850 census extract (Island Creek Township, Jefferson County, Ohio) lists Andrew Ault as male, age 82, occupation farmer, born New York.
The same 1850 household includes Elizabeth Ault (age 83, born New York) and Amy Palmer (age 17, born Ohio).
The extract cites Series M432, Roll 699, Page 523.

Research Gaps

Confirm whether the listed 1850 household member Amy Palmer is a granddaughter, niece, or other relationship.
Verify the 1769 birth and 1852 death values against primary vital/church/cemetery records.
Add residence chronology from additional census years where available.

Sources

Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Census Summary Individuals p1-p10
Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Census Citation Notes
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Census/CensusSummaryIndividual.pdf
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Census/Ancestors in the Census.txt

People/Elizabeth-Plomey-Ault.title: Elizabeth Plomey Ault
People/Elizabeth-Plomey-Ault.content:
Elizabeth Plomey Ault
Biographical Profile

Name: Elizabeth Plomey Ault
Role in this project: Direct ancestor with multi-decade US census entries in the census summary.

Source-Cited Facts

A census-summary entry gives Elizabeth Plomey Ault as born 31 Oct 1841 and died 5 Mar 1926.
The 1850 Salisbury Township, Meigs County, Ohio extract includes Elizabeth Ault age 8 in the Frederick and Mary Ault household.
The 1860 Salisbury Township extract includes Eliza Ault age 18 in a household with Mary Ault and Gilmore family members.
The 1880 Afton Township, Cherokee County, Iowa extract lists Elizabeth Tallman as wife of Miller M. Tallman, with daughter Lena Tallman in the household.
The 1900 Sioux City, Woodbury County, Iowa extract lists Elizabeth P. Tallman with birth month/year Oct 1841.
The 1910 and 1920 Iowa Soldiers’ Home extracts in Marshall County list Elizabeth P. Tallman with Miller M. Tallman.

Research Gaps

Confirm maiden surname and whether all Eliza/Elizabeth Ault entries in these tables are one continuous identity.
Validate all quoted ages and birthplace columns against image-level census pages.
Add spouse and child links only after broader page extraction confirms full household continuity.

Sources

Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Census Summary Individuals p1-p10
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Census/CensusSummaryIndividual.pdf

People/Frederick-Ault.title: Frederick Ault
People/Frederick-Ault.content:
Frederick Ault
Biographical Profile

Name: Frederick Ault
Role in this project: Ault-line ancestor indexed in the census summary and listed as household head in 1850 Meigs County, Ohio.

Source-Cited Facts

A census-summary entry gives Frederick Ault as born 8 Mar 1795 and died about 1857.
The 1850 Salisbury Township, Meigs County, Ohio extract lists Frederick Ault as male, age 55, occupation carpenter, born Pennsylvania.
The same 1850 household includes Mary Ault and children including Elizabeth Ault.

Research Gaps

Confirm that the indexed death estimate c.1857 is supported by independent records.
Reconcile birthplace and household relationships with original census images.
Extend profile with pre-1850 and post-1850 record coverage.

Sources

Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Census Summary Individuals p1-p10
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Census/CensusSummaryIndividual.pdf

People/George-Spicer.title: George Spicer
People/George-Spicer.content:
George Spicer
Biographical Profile

Name: George Spicer
Role in this project: Direct-line ancestor in shared Spicer lineage note.

Source-Cited Facts

The staged lineage chain shows George Spicer paired with Hattie Risden.
The same chain positions George Spicer between Charles Russell Spicer and Lester Harold Spicer.
The census-summary contents index lists SPICER, George B with dates 3 Sep 1864 to 15 May 1938.

Research Gaps

Confirm whether this profile should be merged with a canonical page titled George B. Spicer from timeline/census materials.
Add dates and locations only after direct extraction from timeline PDFs or census-summary pages.
Validate spouse and child links with additional records.

Sources

Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Spicer Lineage Note
Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Census Summary Individuals p1-p10
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Pedigree Timeline/SPICLINE.txt
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Census/CensusSummaryIndividual.pdf

People/Hattie-Risden.content:
Hattie Risden
Biographical Profile

Name: Hattie Risden
Role in this project: Direct-line ancestor in shared Spicer lineage note.

Source-Cited Facts

The staged lineage chain shows Hattie Risden paired with George Spicer.
The same chain places her as parent generation for Lester Harold Spicer.
The census-summary contents index lists RISDEN, Hattie May with dates 29 Mar 1877 to 11 Mar 1967.

Research Gaps

Confirm whether this profile should be merged with canonical timeline naming (for example Hattie May Risden) after source verification.
Add dates and places from timeline/census-summary PDFs once transcribed.
Confirm parentage from additional records.

People/James-Kelly.title: James Kelly
People/James-Kelly.content:
James Kelly
Biographical Profile

Name: James Kelly
Role in this project: Ancestor represented in Peterborough parish/christening extracts and census citation notes.

Source-Cited Facts

A christening extract gives James Kelly christening date as 15 Aug 1830 at Peterborough, Northampton, England.
The same extract names his parents as William Kelly and Sarah.
Census citation notes include a 1871 citation entry for a James Kelly record (RG10 piece/folio/page reference in note text).

Research Gaps

Validate whether the 1871 census citation in notes is a confirmed match to this James Kelly.
Confirm birth date versus christening date from original parish image.
Add spouse/children only after image-level confirmation.

Sources

Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Certificates and Parish Extracts
Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Census Citation Notes
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Certificates/JamesKellyBaptism1830.txt
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Census/EnglishCensusCitations.txt

People/John-Kirby.title: John Kirby
People/John-Kirby.content:
John Kirby
Biographical Profile

Name: John Kirby
Role in this project: Ancestor in Oundle parish-register extracts.

Source-Cited Facts

Marriage extract shows John Kirby married Alice Meadows on 29 May 1752 at St Peter, Oundle, Northamptonshire.
A later marriage extract for William Emblow and Kezia Kirby lists a John Kirby as witness in 1786 at Oundle.

Research Gaps

Confirm whether the 1752 groom and 1786 witness are the same individual.
Resolve whether Peasebrook RUT and Pilton entries indicate residence or parish-of-origin details requiring separate place pages.
Obtain register image copies for witness signature validation.

Sources

Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Certificates and Parish Extracts
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Certificates/JohnKirbyAliceMeadowsMarriage1752.txt
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Certificates/WilliamEmblowKeziaKirbyMarriage1786.txt

People/Kezia-Kirby.title: Kezia Kirby
People/Kezia-Kirby.content:
Kezia Kirby
Biographical Profile

Name: Kezia Kirby
Role in this project: Ancestor in Oundle parish-register extracts linked to Emblow line.

Source-Cited Facts

Marriage extract shows Kezia Kirby married William Emblow on 13 Jul 1786 at St Peter, Oundle, Northamptonshire.
Marriage extract lists John Kirby and Eliz Bellamy as witnesses.

Research Gaps

Confirm Kezia Kirby parentage using earlier Oundle parish entries.
Validate witness relationships before asserting family links.
Add birth/baptism details only after direct-source confirmation.

Sources

Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Certificates and Parish Extracts
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Certificates/WilliamEmblowKeziaKirbyMarriage1786.txt

People/Lenore-Hetty-Tallman.title: Lenore Hetty Tallman
People/Lenore-Hetty-Tallman.content:
Lenore Hetty Tallman
Biographical Profile

Name: Lenore Hetty Tallman
Role in this project: Tallman-line descendant appearing as daughter in late-19th and early-20th century census-summary entries.

Source-Cited Facts

A census-summary entry gives Lenore Hetty Tallman as born 1 Feb 1879 and died 28 Jun 1953.
The 1880 Afton Township, Cherokee County, Iowa extract lists Lena Tallman as daughter, age 1.
The 1900 Sioux City, Woodbury County, Iowa extract lists Lena H. Tallman as daughter, born Feb 1879, age 21, single.

Research Gaps

Confirm whether Lena/Lena H./Lenore Hetty in compiled entries all refer to the same person.
Extend record coverage beyond 1900 and reconcile later-name variants.
Confirm parent-child links from primary census images.

Sources

Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Census Summary Individuals p1-p10
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Census/CensusSummaryIndividual.pdf

People/Lester-Harold-Spicer.title: Lester Harold Spicer
People/Lester-Harold-Spicer.content:
Lester Harold Spicer
Biographical Profile

Name: Lester Harold Spicer
Role in this project: Direct-line ancestor in staged Spicer lineage text.

Source-Cited Facts

The staged lineage chain records Lester Harold Spicer paired with Ruby Bernice Prior.
The same chain places Lester Harold Spicer as parent generation above Karen Lu Spicer.
The census-summary contents index lists SPICER, Lester Harold with dates 14 Jul 1906 to 28 Jun 1974.

Research Gaps

Confirm timeline dates from PedigreeTimelines2025Spicer.pdf before adding to this profile.
Add census and certificate evidence to support relationship assertions.
Resolve whether spelling and middle-name forms are consistent across all source sets.

Sources

Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Spicer Lineage Note
Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Census Summary Individuals p1-p10
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Pedigree Timeline/SPICLINE.txt
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Census/CensusSummaryIndividual.pdf

People/Miller-Mathias-Tallman.title: Miller Mathias Tallman
People/Miller-Mathias-Tallman.content:
Miller Mathias Tallman
Biographical Profile

Name: Miller Mathias Tallman
Role in this project: Tallman-line ancestor connected to Ault-line census households.

Source-Cited Facts

A census-summary entry gives Miller Mathias Tallman as born 14 Apr 1841 and died 8 Apr 1921.
The 1880 Afton Township, Cherokee County, Iowa extract lists Miller M. Tallman as head, married, age 39, occupation farmer, born Ohio.
The 1900 Sioux City, Woodbury County, Iowa extract lists Miller M. Tallman as head, birth month/year Apr 1841, occupation teamster.
The 1910 and 1920 Iowa Soldiers’ Home extracts in Marshall County list Miller M. Tallman as head/member with wife Elizabeth P. Tallman.

Research Gaps

Confirm whether birthplace fields reported as Ohio/Ohio/Ohio in later entries represent corrected data or table normalization.
Add military-service evidence for the Iowa Soldiers’ Home placement.
Validate all household links and ages from page images.

Sources

Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Census Summary Individuals p1-p10

Biographical Profile

People/Ruby-Bernice-Prior.title: Ruby Bernice Prior
People/Ruby-Bernice-Prior.content:
Ruby Bernice Prior
Biographical Profile

Name: Ruby Bernice Prior
Role in this project: Direct-line ancestor in staged Spicer/Prior linkage notes.

Source-Cited Facts

The staged lineage chain records Ruby Bernice Prior paired with Lester Harold Spicer.
The same chain places Ruby Bernice Prior in the parent generation of Karen Lu Spicer.
The census-summary contents index lists PRIOR, Ruby Bernice with dates 24 Apr 1913 to 1 Aug 2006.

Research Gaps

Confirm dates from timeline and census-summary PDFs before adding them here.
Correlate with Prior branch records to confirm parentage.
Add location data from direct sources.

People/William-Emblow.content:
William Emblow
Biographical Profile

Name: William Emblow
Role in this project: Ancestor in Emblow branch represented in Oundle marriage extract.

Source-Cited Facts

Marriage extract shows William Emblow married Kezia Kirby on 13 Jul 1786 at St Peter, Oundle, Northamptonshire.
Census citation notes in intake files include Emblow-surname entries in later UK census references.

Research Gaps

Resolve identity linkage between this William Emblow and later Emblow individuals in census notes.
Confirm birth/baptism and death details from parish and civil sources.
Verify whether witness Eliz Bellamy indicates a Bellamy-family connection or incidental witness.

People/William-Henry-Palmer.title: William Henry Palmer
People/William-Henry-Palmer.content:
William Henry Palmer
Biographical Profile

Name: William Henry Palmer
Role in this project: Ancestor represented in death-index extract and census-summary materials.

Source-Cited Facts

Intake death-record text notes a death-index entry (not a full certificate image) for William H. Palmer in Waterloo, Iowa, dated 8 May 1927, age 75, male, white, married, with cause listed as acute bronchial pneumonia.
Repository contact in the source note identifies Blackhawk County Recorder, Vital Records, Waterloo, Iowa.

Research Gaps

Obtain the full death certificate image and confirm all transcribed fields.
Correlate this death-index entry with census-summary page references before asserting household relationships.
Confirm birth details and parentage from additional records.

Sources

Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Certificates and Parish Extracts
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Certificates/CERT0058PalmerWilliaHenry-Death Record.txt

People/William-Kelly.title: William Kelly
People/William-Kelly.content:
William Kelly
Biographical Profile

Name: William Kelly
Role in this project: Ancestor in the Kelly branch connected through Peterborough parish records.

Source-Cited Facts

A christening-record extract for James Kelly identifies William Kelly as father, with spouse Sarah, in Peterborough, Northampton, England.
A burial-record extract gives a William Kelly burial date of 06 Mar 1840 in Peterborough, Northamptonshire, with age listed as 52.

Research Gaps

Confirm whether the William Kelly named in the 1830 christening extract and the 1840 burial extract are the same person.
Obtain image-level register copies for both entries.
Establish a birth range and parentage with additional sources.

Sources

Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Certificates and Parish Extracts
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Certificates/JamesKellyBaptism1830.txt
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Certificates/WilliamKellyBurial1840.txt

References/Butch-Thorpe-Email.title: Butch Thorpe Email
References/Butch-Thorpe-Email.content:
Butch Thorpe Email
Source file: References/raw/butch-thorpe-email.txt
Sender and Context

Sender signs as Butch (nickname for Robert “Butch” Thorpe).
Message sent to Zach with interpretation guidance for attached pedigree timeline files.

Extracted Statements

Attachments are intended as a resource-finding aid and include pedigrees.
Horizontal bars represent ancestor lifespans.
Not all ancestors with records are shown; only those from earliest generations that fit on the page.
Red dots denote official record types, with nearby numbers referencing Butch’s personal copies.
Blue dots denote census records at timeline dates.
Butch can provide a PDF describing the record types he has collected.

Notes
This email provides interpretation metadata for the .cdr pedigree files and should be cited when describing how timeline markers are encoded.

References/Shared-Intake-2026-04-22-Cens.title: Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Census Citation Notes
References/Shared-Intake-2026-04-22-Cens.content:
Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Census Citation Notes
Source files:

References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Census/EnglishCensusCitations.txt
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Census/English Census Notes.txt
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Census/Ancestors in the Census.txt

Context

Files were staged from shared intake in text-note form.
These notes compile citation references for UK and selected US census appearances tied to Thorpe-line ancestors.

Extracted Statements

The notes include UK citation references for 1841, 1861, 1871, and 1901 census years using Class/Piece/Folio/Page formats (for example HO107, RG9, RG10, RG13).
Individuals listed include Bellamy, Sorrell, Thorogood, Emblow, Kelly, Whitfield, and Munson/Monson variants.
The notes include US citation entries for 1900 with roll/page/enumeration district formats and names including Clyde Thorpe and Joseph Prior.
English Census Notes.txt links individual image filenames to citation metadata, indicating potential image-level validation paths.

Uncertainty Notes

Person-name spelling varies across files, including Monson/Munson and Witfield/Whitfield.
Notes include possible or inferred matches in some filename annotations and should be treated as provisional until image checks are complete.

References/Shared-Intake-2026-04-22-Cens.filePath: References/Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Census Summary Individuals p1-p10.md
References/Shared-Intake-2026-04-22-Cens.title: Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Census Summary Individuals p1-p10
References/Shared-Intake-2026-04-22-Cens.content:
Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Census Summary Individuals p1-p10
Source files:

References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Census/CensusSummaryIndividual.pdf
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Census/Ancestors in the Census.txt
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Census/EnglishCensusCitations.txt

Processing scope

Parsed and reviewed pages 1-10 of CensusSummaryIndividual.pdf.
This is a partial extraction pass focused on high-confidence entries in early pages.

Extracted statements

The document title page identifies the work as a census summary for individual ancestors of Lancelot and Wendolyn Thorpe, compiled by Robert Thorpe in October 2009.
The introduction states the intended census coverage range is 1841 through 1930 and that the index lists direct ancestors.
The contents pages include direct-ancestor entries for AULT, Andrew, AULT, Elizabeth Plomey, and AULT, Frederick.
Page 9 includes an 1850 Island Creek Township, Jefferson County, Ohio household for Andrew Ault (age 82, farmer, born New York) with Elizabeth Ault (age 83, born New York) and Amy Palmer (age 17, born Ohio), cited to Series M432, Roll 699, Page 523.
Page 10 includes an Elizabeth Plomey Ault section with census entries for 1850, 1860, 1880, 1900, 1910, and 1920, including repeated Tallman-household entries for Miller M. Tallman and Elizabeth P. Tallman.
Page 10 entries identify Lena/Lena H. Tallman as daughter in 1880 and 1900 records and include birthplace fields and household relationships.

Uncertainty notes

OCR/parsing may contain minor transcription artifacts in abbreviations and column headers.
Extracted statements are limited to pages 1-10 and do not yet include deeper person-specific pages after page 10.
Compiler contact details visible in the introduction were not propagated to person pages.

References/Shared-Intake-2026-04-22-Cert.filePath: References/Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Certificates and Parish Extracts.md
References/Shared-Intake-2026-04-22-Cert.title: Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Certificates and Parish Extracts
References/Shared-Intake-2026-04-22-Cert.content:
Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Certificates and Parish Extracts
Source files:

References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Certificates/CERT0058PalmerWilliaHenry-Death Record.txt
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Certificates/JamesKellyBaptism1830.txt
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Certificates/WilliamKellyBurial1840.txt
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Certificates/JohnKirbyAliceMeadowsMarriage1752.txt
References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Certificates/WilliamEmblowKeziaKirbyMarriage1786.txt

Context

Files were staged from the shared intake crawl dated 2026-04-22.
These are text extracts and index/transcription notes rather than complete image certificates.

Extracted Statements

CERT0058PalmerWilliaHenry-Death Record.txt states there is no attached certificate image and records a death-index entry for William H. Palmer in Waterloo, Iowa on 8 May 1927, age 75, with cause listed as acute bronchial pneumonia.
JamesKellyBaptism1830.txt gives James Kelly christening date as 15 Aug 1830 at Peterborough, Northampton, England, with parents William Kelly and Sarah.
WilliamKellyBurial1840.txt gives a burial entry for William Kelly on 06 Mar 1840 in Peterborough, age 52.
JohnKirbyAliceMeadowsMarriage1752.txt gives marriage entry date 29 May 1752 in Oundle, Northamptonshire for John Kirby and Alice Meadows.
WilliamEmblowKeziaKirbyMarriage1786.txt gives marriage entry date 13 Jul 1786 in Oundle, Northamptonshire for William Emblow and Kezia Kirby, with witness names John Kirby and Eliz Bellamy.

Uncertainty Notes

Several entries are transcribed/indexed summaries and may not reflect full image-side details.
Name spellings and abbreviations should be verified against original register images when available.

References/Shared-Intake-2026-04-22-Spic.title: Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Spicer Lineage Note
References/Shared-Intake-2026-04-22-Spic.content:
Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Spicer Lineage Note
Source file: References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Pedigree Timeline/SPICLINE.txt
Context

File was staged from Pedigree Timeline/Misc Pedigree Timeline Files/SPICLINE.txt.
It is a compact lineage chain text from early Spicer ancestors to Karen Lu Spicer.

Extracted Lineage Chain

Peter Busecot + Mary [unknown]
Peter Spicer + Mary Busecot
Jabez Spicer + Margaret Parke
Nathan Spicer + Leah [unknown]
Nathan Spicer + Catherine Filkins
Nathan Spicer + Claramon Tiffany
Charles Russell Spicer + Mary Burgett
George Spicer + Hattie Risden
Lester Harold Spicer + Ruby Bernice Prior
Karen Lu Spicer + Robert AJ Thorpe

Uncertainty Notes

The file provides a linear chain and omits dates/places in this text version.
Some spouse surnames are blank or partially unknown and should remain explicitly marked unknown.

Topics/Thorpe-Pedigree-Timelines.title: Thorpe Pedigree Timelines
Topics/Thorpe-Pedigree-Timelines.content:
Thorpe Pedigree Timelines
This page tracks the pedigree timeline files provided by Robert “Butch” Thorpe and documents what they contain.
Files Provided

References/raw/PedigreeTimelines2025.cdr
References/raw/PedigreeTimelines2019Descendants2.cdr

Butch’s Notes on How to Read the Timelines
Per Butch’s email:

Horizontal bars represent ancestor lifespans.
Red dots indicate official records.
Numbers by red dots refer to Butch’s personal record copies.
Blue dots indicate census records corresponding to the date marked by vertical lines.
The charts show selected early generations that fit on each page, not all ancestors with records.

Current Limitation
The files are CorelDRAW (.cdr) documents and require export to PDF or image before structured transcription into person pages can proceed.
Requested Follow-up Sources

PDF list of record types collected by Butch
Mapping/key for numbered red-dot record references

index.title: Thorpe Family History
index.content:
The Thorpe Family
This vault is the starting point for a dedicated Thorpe family research project.
Maintained by Zach Copley with source contributions from Robert “Butch” Thorpe.
Latest Deployment
Latest deployed update: 2026-04-22 intake batch-1 ingestion.

See Changelog for full change history.
See Agent Handoff — Phase 1B for staged sources, added pages, and next steps.
Quick links to new intake outputs:

Certificates and Parish Extracts
Census Citation Notes
Spicer Lineage Note



Where to Start

Home
People Directory
Search Index
Thorpe Pedigree Timelines
Butch Thorpe Email
CHANGELOG

Current Research Status
The initial source package contains:

An explanatory email from Butch
Two CorelDRAW pedigree timeline files (.cdr)

The .cdr files require CorelDRAW-compatible viewing/export before detailed names, dates, and relationships can be transcribed into person pages.
